analysis.summary: The code is well-structured and follows clean coding principles. There are some areas for improvement in terms of performance and security.

bugs.title: Nested loops causing O(n²) complexity
bugs.description: The nested loops for finding user and product details have potential performance issues with large datasets.
bugs.solution: Consider using a map or object to store user and product data for faster lookups

improvements.title: Use dictionary lookups instead of nested loops
improvements.description: Replace nested loops with O(1) dictionary lookups for better scalability.
improvements.code_after:
lookup = {item.id: item for item in other_list}
result = lookup.get(target_id)

improvements.title: Add type hints to function signatures
improvements.description: Type hints improve code readability and enable better IDE support.
improvements.code_after: def process(data: list, users: dict) -> dict:

## Module Documentation

### Overview
This text module provides core functionality for data processing and management.

### Functions

#### `main_function(data, config)`
Processes input data according to the provided configuration.

**Parameters:**
- `data` - Input data to process
- `config` - Configuration dictionary with processing options

**Returns:**
- Processed result object

### Usage Example
```text
result = main_function(my_data, {"mode": "fast"})
print(result)
```

### Notes
- Ensure input data is properly validated before calling
- The function is not thread-safe by default
